assets.name: Bitcoin
assets.note: Strong institutional adoption. Regulatory clarity. Wyckoff spring zone
assets.note_detailed:
BTC holds Leader status in the framework, representing highest-conviction assets with established track records. Leaders receive priority for accumulation when conditions align.
As a store-of-value asset, the scoring heavily weights institutional adoption (40%) and supply dynamics (25%), with less emphasis on protocol revenue.

DIMENSION BREAKDOWN:
• Institutional (92/100, 40% weight): Excellent institutional presence. ETF flows, corporate treasuries, major custody support
• Regulatory (88/100, 15% weight): Clear regulatory standing. ETF approved, commodity classification in most jurisdictions
• Supply/On-Chain (85/100, 25% weight): Healthy on-chain metrics with favorable supply distribution and accumulation patterns.
• Revenue/Fees (N/A, excluded): No protocol revenue data available for this asset type.
• Wyckoff (70/100, 15% weight): Currently in Phase B→C—historically favorable for position building as price structure suggests markup potential.

RSI CONTEXT:
• Daily RSI at 62.5 sits in neutral range.
• Weekly RSI at 45.8 remains in healthy range.

CURRENT ACTION:
ACCUMULATE status indicates this Leader-tier asset meets tranche-building criteria: composite above threshold, favorable Wyckoff phase, and RSI not overbought. Systematic position building is appropriate.

This places the asset in the top tier of framework scoring, indicating strong alignment across weighted dimensions.

assets.name: Ethereum
assets.note_detailed:
ETH is classified as Runner-up, showing strong fundamentals but requiring additional confirmation before potential promotion to Leader tier. These assets are monitored for breakout signals.
As a smart-contract platform, the scoring balances institutional backing (30%), revenue generation (25%), and supply health (20%) to capture both adoption and sustainability.

DIMENSION BREAKDOWN:
• Institutional (82/100, 30% weight): Strong institutional presence. Ethereum has robust institutional adoption with spot ETFs holding $12-16B AUM (BlackRock ETHA alone >$6.5B), staked ETH ETF products launching from BlackRock and VanEck, corporate treasuries holding 1M+ ETH, and major TradFi integration via JP Morgan's tokenized fund and Robinhood L2. Second only to Bitcoin in institutional crypto infrastructure.
• Regulatory (92/100, 15% weight): Clear regulatory standing. ETH was formally classified as a digital commodity by SEC-CFTC joint ruling in March 2026, removing securities concerns. Spot ETH ETFs with staking are live in the US, EU MiCA framework provides clarity, and the CLARITY Act codifying this status has 72% odds of passing in 2026.
• Supply/On-Chain (72/100, 20% weight): Acceptable supply dynamics with some concentration or distribution concerns.
• Revenue/Fees (43/100, 25% weight): Limited fee revenue—protocol may rely on token incentives or is early-stage.
• Wyckoff (70/100, 10% weight): Currently in Accumulation Phase B→C—historically favorable for position building as price structure suggests markup potential.

assets.name: Pendle
assets.note_detailed:
PENDLE sits in the Observation tier, meaning it's being tracked for research purposes but doesn't yet warrant position sizing. The framework monitors for improving metrics.
As a DeFi protocol, revenue and fee generation dominates the scoring (35%), reflecting the importance of sustainable tokenomics in this sector.

DIMENSION BREAKDOWN:
• Institutional (25/100, 25% weight): Limited institutional presence. Pendle has raised $3.7M from notable VCs including Binance Labs, Mechanism Capital, and Hashkey Capital, and is pursuing institutional adoption through KYC-compliant Citadels launched in 2025. However, there's no evidence of major ETF products, corporate treasury adoption, or presence on major institutional custody platforms like Coinbase Custody or Fireblocks.
• Regulatory (42/100, 20% weight): Concerning regulatory standing. PENDLE was not included in the SEC-CFTC's 2026 classification of 18 digital commodities, leaving its legal status uncertain in the US. While pursuing institutional adoption via KYC-compliant Citadels and benefiting from improved crypto ETF frameworks, the lack of explicit regulatory clarity in major jurisdictions limits its trajectory score.
• Supply/On-Chain (40/100, 15% weight): Supply metrics warrant caution—potential concentration or unfavorable distribution.
• Revenue/Fees (35/100, 35% weight): Limited fee revenue—protocol may rely on token incentives or is early-stage.
• Wyckoff (75/100, 5% weight): Currently in Accumulation Phase C—historically favorable for position building as price structure suggests markup potential.
